metadata.title: Why Measuring Sideways Erases What You Knew
metadata.note: Clean rebuild. Prepare z+, perform x measurement, then z measurement; final z outcomes are 50/50. Explicitly conditions on either x branch or treats the x result as unread.

source_of_truth: beat_sheet.json — edit the sheet, not this file

beats.label: This is Liam, in for Bear. Prepare every spin one-half particle as definitely up along z. Measure si

beats.prompt: First prepare z up. Send a beam through a z analyzer and keep only the upper output. A second z measurement would now return up with certainty.
beats.label: First prepare z up. Send a beam through a z analyzer and keep only the upper output. A second z meas

beats.prompt: How can a sideways measurement destroy that certainty without applying a random classical kick?
beats.label: How can a sideways measurement destroy that certainty without applying a random classical kick?

beats.prompt: The state z up is not definite along x. In the x basis it is an equal superposition of x plus and x minus.
beats.label: The state z up is not definite along x. In the x basis it is an equal superposition of x plus and x

beats.prompt: An x measurement therefore produces x plus or x minus with equal probability. The measurement prepares whichever x eigenstate its outcome reports.
beats.label: An x measurement therefore produces x plus or x minus with equal probability. The measurement prepar

beats.prompt: Take the x plus branch. Relative to z, x plus is an equal superposition of z up and z down. A final z analyzer must split it fifty-fifty.
beats.label: Take the x plus branch. Relative to z, x plus is an equal superposition of z up and z down. A final

beats.prompt: Compare the sequences. Z followed directly by Z gives one hundred percent up. Z, then X, then Z gives fifty percent up and fifty percent down.
beats.label: Compare the sequences. Z followed directly by Z gives one hundred percent up. Z, then X, then Z give

beats.prompt: The middle measurement does not reveal a hidden sideways value while preserving z. It changes the state into an x eigenstate, which has no definite z value.
beats.label: The middle measurement does not reveal a hidden sideways value while preserving z. It changes the st

beats.prompt: Measurement order matters because spin components along different axes are incompatible observables. Certainty in one basis becomes superposition in the other.
beats.label: Measurement order matters because spin components along different axes are incompatible observables.

beats.prompt: Rotate the middle analyzer gradually. At zero degrees, z certainty survives. At ninety degrees, the final z result is maximally randomized. Intermediate angles
beats.label: Rotate the middle analyzer gradually. At zero degrees, z certainty survives. At ninety degrees, the

beats.prompt: Z up was certain. X made a new state. The last Z measurement reveals the lost compatibility as a fifty-fifty split.
